seo.title: Step-Up SIP to ₹10 Crore – Pooja Bhinde on Money Psychology
seo.description: In this episode of This or That with Bhartendra Singh, I sit down with Pooja Bhinde, Certified Financial Planner, to uncover the truth about money. We

takeaways.text: Chasing returns is the wrong place to start; CFP Pooja Bhinde and Bhartendra frame wealth as a question of psychology, discipline and planning, not hot tips or products.

Time and compounding do the real work, and the episode's headline example is the step-up SIP, where raising your contribution as your income grows is shown taking a ₹10,000 monthly investment toward the ₹10 crore mark over a long horizon.
A real financial plan is wider than investing: budgeting, cash flow, a contingency fund, insurance and staying clear of debt traps all sit inside it.
Building money is not just for the wealthy, illustrated by real stories that run from a house help's SIP to financial security for senior citizens.
Women taking part in financial decisions is treated as essential to a household's stability, not an optional extra.
